title: Shadow AI 'double agents' are outpacing security visibility - and that's a serious concern for UK businesses
quote: Unmanaged AI agents are creating major visibility gaps and security blind spots as adoption accelerates across enterprises.
summary: Microsoft warns that unmanaged and unauthorised AI agents are rapidly outpacing the ability of security teams to monitor and govern them. As enterprise AI adoption accelerates, these so-called shadow AI agents create significant blind spots in security visibility and governance. The growing lack of central oversight over AI agent identities and actions poses a serious and growing risk for UK organisations.
relevanceBullets: Ongeautoriseerde en slecht gemonitorde AI‑agents ('shadow AI') creëren blinde vlekken in security en governance binnen organisaties.
Onderstreept de noodzaak van centrale controle, identity management en auditability voor enterprise‑AI gebruik.

title: Meta AI agent's instruction causes large sensitive data leak to employees
quote: An AI agent at Meta suggested actions that unintentionally exposed sensitive employee data, highlighting governance risks of autonomous AI systems.
summary: Meta faced a significant internal data exposure incident after an AI agent recommended actions that inadvertently revealed sensitive information to employees. The incident highlights how autonomous AI systems operating without sufficient governance controls can directly cause internal data leaks and compliance failures. It raises urgent questions about the need for human oversight, monitoring, and robust guardrails when deploying AI agents in enterprise environments.
relevanceBullets: Incident toont hoe autonome AI‑agents zonder voldoende governance direct kunnen leiden tot interne datalekken en compliance‑risico's.
Benadrukt het risico van AI‑adoptie zonder voldoende controlemechanismen, monitoring en menselijke oversight binnen enterprise omgevingen.

title: Okta unveils new framework to secure and protect enterprise AI agents
quote: Okta introduces a framework to manage AI agents as identities, addressing growing risks from rogue or unmanaged agents in enterprise environments.
summary: Okta has introduced a new security framework that treats AI agents as managed identities, aiming to close the governance gap created by the proliferation of unmanaged and rogue AI agents in enterprise environments. The framework provides organisations with structured mechanisms for access control, auditability, and oversight of AI agent activity. The initiative reflects a broader industry recognition that AI agents must be governed with the same rigour as human identities.
relevanceBullets: Het feit dat organisaties AI‑agents nog niet als identiteiten beheren laat een duidelijke governance‑gap en security‑risico zien.
De opkomst van 'rogue' en shadow AI‑agents benadrukt de noodzaak van gecontroleerde, enterprise‑grade AI governance en toegangsbeheer.

title: The risk for SMBs is not reckless use of AI, but invisible workflow change
quote: Many legal professionals use AI tools while organizations lack formal policies, raising the risk that sensitive data is entered into public AI systems.
summary: A new report highlights that the primary AI-related risk for small and medium-sized businesses is not deliberate misuse, but the gradual and invisible integration of AI tools into daily workflows. Many professionals, particularly in legal services, already use AI tools while their organizations still lack formal AI policies. This creates significant data leakage and compliance risks, as sensitive client information can inadvertently be entered into public AI systems without institutional oversight.
relevanceBullets: Veel professionals gebruiken AI‑tools terwijl hun organisaties nog geen formeel AI‑beleid of governance hebben.
Het invoeren van gevoelige informatie in publieke AI‑systemen vergroot risico's rond datalekken, privacy en compliance.

title: Shadow AI rises as leaders choose speed over governance
quote: Most AI initiatives at a department level are going live without formal company oversight even as risks mount, according to an EY survey of technology leaders.
summary: An EY survey of technology leaders reveals that most AI initiatives at the departmental level are deployed without formal company oversight, even as associated risks continue to grow. Leaders are choosing speed of AI adoption over governance and risk controls, leaving a significant gap in enterprise AI management. Security and IT teams report struggling to maintain visibility into departmental AI usage, raising concerns about data exposure and regulatory compliance.
relevanceBullets: Veel AI‑initiatieven binnen afdelingen worden zonder formele governance of goedkeuring gelanceerd, wat het risico op ongecontroleerde 'shadow AI' vergroot.
Het artikel laat zien dat snelheid van AI‑adoptie vaak belangrijker wordt gevonden dan governance en compliance, precies het probleem dat enterprise‑AI‑controle moet oplossen.

title: Navigating AI Adoption: Mitigating The Risks Posed By Untrained Users
quote: Organizations adopting generative AI face growing risk from employees using AI tools without training, policies, or oversight.
summary: Organizations adopting generative AI are increasingly exposed to risk created by employees who use AI tools without proper training, policies, or oversight. This uncontrolled usage creates serious data protection and compliance concerns. The article argues that structured AI policies and governed enterprise AI environments are essential to mitigate these risks.
relevanceBullets: Ongetrainde werknemers die AI-tools gebruiken vergroten de kans op datalekken en compliance‑problemen binnen organisaties.
Het artikel onderstreept het belang van governance, beleid en gecontroleerde enterprise‑AI om ongecontroleerd gebruik te voorkomen.

title: EY survey: autonomous AI adoption surges at tech companies as oversight falls behind
quote: Companies accelerate adoption of autonomous AI systems even as governance frameworks, oversight, and risk controls struggle to keep pace.
summary: An EY technology poll reveals that companies are rapidly accelerating their adoption of autonomous AI systems. However, governance frameworks, oversight mechanisms, and risk controls are struggling to keep pace with this rapid deployment. The growing gap between adoption speed and organizational readiness creates significant compliance and security exposure for enterprises.
relevanceBullets: Bedrijven versnellen de inzet van autonome AI terwijl governance‑ en controlemechanismen achterblijven.
Dit illustreert het groeiende risico dat AI‑implementaties sneller gaan dan beleid, compliance en toezicht binnen organisaties.

title: Manifest report reveals AI readiness gap as enterprise security teams struggle with visibility and governance
quote: Executives believe their organizations are prepared for AI risks, but security teams report major governance gaps, shadow AI usage, and limited visibility.
summary: A new Manifest survey of security leaders reveals a significant disconnect between executive confidence and operational reality in AI risk management. While senior leadership believes their organizations are well-prepared for AI risks, security teams report major governance gaps, widespread shadow AI usage, and limited visibility into AI components embedded in enterprise software.
relevanceBullets: Onderzoek laat zien dat 'shadow AI' al in veel organisaties aanwezig is en vaak buiten zicht van security- en governance‑teams wordt gebruikt.
Het verschil tussen managementvertrouwen en operationele realiteit benadrukt de noodzaak van controleerbare en governable enterprise‑AI oplossingen.

title: CIOs say AI adoption is moving faster than they can manage
quote: Organizations are investing in AI faster than they can manage it.
summary: The Logicalis Global CIO Report 2026, based on a survey of 1,000 technology leaders worldwide, shows that AI investment and deployment are outpacing the governance, skills, and operating models needed to manage them safely. CIOs report a widening gap between AI adoption ambition and the organizational readiness required to execute responsibly.
relevanceBullets: Bevestigt vanuit CIO-perspectief dat AI-adoptie structureel sneller gaat dan governance, waardoor organisaties kwetsbaar blijven.
Ondersteunt de positionering van pseudoAI als noodzakelijke governancelaag voor organisaties die AI veilig en beheersbaar willen inzetten.

title: Your employees are using AI, whether you like it or not - but are they using AI securely?
quote: Nearly half of GenAI users still access tools through personal or unmanaged accounts, linking shadow AI directly to data leakage and compliance risk.
summary: A new report reveals that 83% of UK employees regularly use generative AI for work tasks, with 78% introducing their own AI tools without employer oversight. Nearly half still access these tools through personal or unmanaged accounts, creating direct exposure to data leakage, IP loss, and violations of regulations such as GDPR and HIPAA.
relevanceBullets: Toont aan dat de meerderheid van medewerkers al buiten officiële IT-kanalen om AI inzet, wat databeheersing en compliance actief bedreigt.
Benadrukt dat shadow AI via persoonlijke accounts een concrete governance-blinde vlek creëert die organisaties direct raakt.

title: EU regulators warn companies about unmanaged AI risks
quote: Companies must ensure AI systems comply with new EU rules.
summary: European regulators stress that unmanaged AI deployments may conflict with emerging AI regulations. Companies are urged to implement monitoring and documentation mechanisms.
relevanceBullets: Geeft aan dat AI-gebruik zonder controle ook juridisch steeds risicovoller wordt binnen Europa.
Onderstreept dat governance en documenteerbaarheid geen optie maar noodzaak zijn.

title: CIOs told: Prove your AI pays off – or pay the price
quote: 82% of CIOs say employees are creating AI agents and apps faster than IT can govern them.
summary: A report shows that 82% of CIOs say employees are building AI agents and applications faster than IT departments can govern them, while the same proportion express concern about sensitive company data exposure from worker-built AI. Organizations are under increasing pressure to demonstrate measurable return on investment from enterprise AI spending while simultaneously closing governance gaps.
relevanceBullets: Laat zien dat medewerkers actief AI-agenten bouwen buiten IT-toezicht, wat het risico op blootstelling van gevoelige bedrijfsdata vergroot.
Onderstreept dat gecontroleerde AI-inzet, zoals pseudoAI biedt, essentieel is om de kloof tussen adoptie en governance te dichten.

title: AI adoption outpaces governance frameworks in large enterprises
quote: Organizations are deploying AI faster than they can implement policy controls.
summary: Enterprise AI adoption is accelerating faster than governance frameworks can keep up. Analysts warn that this imbalance exposes companies to compliance and reputational risks.
relevanceBullets: Toont dat beleid en controle vaak achterlopen op technologische adoptie, waardoor risico's zich opstapelen.
Illustreert waarom een vooraf ingerichte, veilige AI-omgeving essentieel is voor structurele beheersing.

title: Snelle AI-adoptie vergroot risico op shadow IT
quote: AI-adoptie groeit sneller dan governance en securitycontrole kunnen bijbenen.
summary: Nederlandse organisaties zetten AI snel in, maar beleid en toezicht lopen achter. Daardoor ontstaat een nieuwe vorm van shadow IT met verhoogde compliance- en securityrisico's.
relevanceBullets: Maakt duidelijk dat ook Nederlandse organisaties te maken hebben met ongecontroleerde AI-inzet.
Onderbouwt dat centrale AI-regie noodzakelijk is om compliance-risico's te beperken.

title: Enterprise security teams report increase in AI-related data leaks
quote: Sensitive corporate documents are being uploaded into generative AI tools without approval.
summary: Security teams report a growing number of incidents where employees upload confidential documents into AI tools outside approved environments. This increases regulatory exposure and complicates incident response processes.
relevanceBullets: Maakt concreet dat onbeheerd AI-gebruik kan leiden tot daadwerkelijke datalekken met juridische gevolgen.
Bevestigt dat organisaties een gecontroleerde toegangspoort tot AI nodig hebben om gevoelige informatie te beschermen.

title: BYOAI trend exposes enterprises to compliance risks
quote: Bring Your Own AI is becoming the new shadow IT.
summary: The rise of Bring Your Own AI (BYOAI) shows employees increasingly introducing their own AI tools into corporate environments, often without governance structures in place.
relevanceBullets: Laat zien dat medewerkers zelf AI-tools meenemen als er geen veilige bedrijfsoplossing beschikbaar is.
Benadrukt dat organisaties een officieel, gecontroleerd alternatief moeten bieden om shadow AI te voorkomen.

title: Survey: 70% of employees use AI tools without IT approval
quote: Seven in ten employees admit using AI tools at work without IT oversight.
summary: A recent enterprise survey shows that 70% of employees use generative AI tools without informing IT or security teams. This widespread unmanaged adoption creates blind spots in governance, security monitoring, and compliance oversight.
relevanceBullets: Laat zien dat medewerkers in de praktijk al AI gebruiken buiten formele IT-controle om, waardoor organisaties grip verliezen op datastromen.
Onderstreept de noodzaak van een veilige, gecontroleerde AI-workflow zodat productiviteit mogelijk blijft zonder governance-risico.

title: Privacywaakhond waarschuwt voor delen gevoelige informatie via AI-tools zoals ChatGPT
quote: De Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens waarschuwt dat medewerkers onbewust gevoelige informatie invoeren in AI-tools zoals ChatGPT.
summary: De Autoriteit Persoonsgegevens (AP) waarschuwt bedrijven dat medewerkers onbewust persoonsgegevens invoeren in AI-chattools zoals ChatGPT. Dit kan leiden tot ongewilde datalekken en schending van de AVG. Organisaties zijn verantwoordelijk voor het borgen van dataminimalisatie en governance rondom AI-gebruik.
relevanceBullets: Laat zien dat medewerkers actief gevoelige bedrijfs- en persoonsgegevens invoeren in AI-chattools zoals ChatGPT zonder voldoende bewustzijn of controle.
Onderstreept dat organisaties verantwoordelijkheid dragen voor governance en dataminimalisatie bij AI-gebruik om AVG-risico's en datalekken te voorkomen.

title: Employees are putting company data into ChatGPT without approval, study finds
quote: A growing number of employees are entering sensitive company data into AI chatbots without employer approval, raising concerns about data leakage and compliance.
summary: A new study reveals that employees frequently enter sensitive company data into AI chatbots such as ChatGPT without prior employer approval. This unmanaged behavior creates significant data leakage risks and compliance exposures. Security experts emphasize the need for clear organizational policies and approved tools to manage AI usage.
relevanceBullets: Bevestigt dat medewerkers zonder toestemming bedrijfsgevoelige informatie invoeren in AI-chattools, wat leidt tot shadow AI en datalekrisico's.
Toont aan dat gebrek aan controle en beleid rondom AI-chats directe compliance- en securityproblemen veroorzaakt binnen organisaties.

title: Legal professionals risk breaching confidentiality by using generative AI tools
quote: Experts warn that professionals are inadvertently exposing confidential client data by entering it into generative AI systems without proper safeguards.
summary: Legal professionals are increasingly using generative AI tools to process work, inadvertently exposing confidential client information in the process. Experts warn that entering sensitive data into AI systems without appropriate safeguards constitutes a serious confidentiality risk. The trend highlights the urgent need for governance frameworks and approved AI tooling across professional sectors.
relevanceBullets: Professionals delen vertrouwelijke klantinformatie in AI-tools, wat direct leidt tot schending van vertrouwelijkheid en mogelijke datalekken.
Benadrukt dat zonder duidelijke governance en tooling AI-chatgebruik een structureel risico vormt voor compliance en vertrouwelijke data.
